story.text: As a managing session and as bdo, I want a deterministic read-only fold that, given one arc, maps its work-topology — arc -> epic -> story -> task -> environment -> node -> occupant, each rung carrying state, evidence (record ids), and a next safe move — so the system knows where work is, what level it is at, what it is a child of, who occupies which node and by whose authority, and what is safe to do without taking bdo's authority. This is the first real Field: the map the portal will later render. Where a lower rung is not first-class on disk yet, the fold surfaces it as a named gap rather than inventing it; an occupant with no realness admission renders as un-authorised.

_note: The first piece of epic.the-field (reframed from monitor to work-topology by the 2026-06-11 outside go/no-go review). Its done-line is 0050. It serves a confirmed-only arc: until bdo confirms epic.the-field, this atom parks at owner-stamp by design (the arc's pieces flow on his standing confirmation, done-line 0028). loop/gaps.py is the proof-of-shape the build generalises; this atom touches a new loop/field.py and hands off to the confirm seam. Authored as the capacity for bdo's overnight loop to take action on — the contract (0050) is the bar; this atom is the pipeline item.
